tableDataModels.description: Customer export dataset containing one record per customer with demographic, contact, location, and financial profile attributes. Includes surrogate identifiers for geographic entities (city, state/province, country) and segmentation fields (income level, credit limit).

columns.description: Unique identifier for the customer within this export; used as the primary key to reference the customer in downstream systems.
Customer’s given/first name as provided in the source data.
Customer’s family/last name as provided in the source data.
Customer’s gender code (for example, M or F). May contain other codes or be null if unspecified.
Four-digit year the customer was born (e.g., 1960). Useful for deriving age; not a full date of birth.
Customer’s marital status as a text value (e.g., married). May be null if unknown.
Primary street address line for the customer, as free-form text.
Postal or ZIP code for the address, stored numerically. Note: numeric storage may omit leading zeros; some regions may use non-numeric postal codes.
City or town name associated with the customer’s address.
Surrogate identifier for the city; used to join to a city/geo reference or dimension table.
State, province, or region name for the customer’s address.
Surrogate identifier for the state or province; used to join to a state/province reference or dimension table.
Surrogate identifier for the country; used to join to a country reference or dimension table.
Primary phone number for the customer, stored as text to preserve formatting (e.g., dashes or spaces).
Income bracket label for the customer (e.g., 'L: 300,000 and above'). Used for segmentation and reporting.
Credit limit amount associated with the customer, stored as a whole number. Currency is not specified in this table.
Primary email address for the customer.
Text descriptor for a customer-level aggregation/category used in reporting. In sample data this appears as 'Customer total'; pairs with CUST_TOTAL_ID.
Identifier corresponding to the CUST_TOTAL descriptor; used for grouping or joining to a lookup of aggregation labels.
Source system identifier for the customer record (e.g., originating system key or external reference). May be null if not provided.
Effective start date of the customer record version (the date from which the row becomes valid). Stored as a string in a day-month-year format (e.g., 1-Jan-98).

Effective end date of the customer record version (the date through which the row remains valid). Null indicates an open-ended/current record. Stored as a string in a day-month-year format if present.
Validity status flag for the record version. Typical values: 'A' = active/current version, 'I' = inactive/historical (superseded).
